title: Installing Verilator configured with --enable-ccwarn breaks (some) production use cases
body:
Steps to reproduce:

1. A gcc version subject to bug #69602
     https://gcc.gnu.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=69602
This includes Debian Buster's gcc 8.3.0 and Debian Bullseye's gcc 10.2.1.

2. A Verilator test bench containing C code that triggers the above bug. Commonly seen compiling networking code (on Linux) that includes the construct
  `if (errno == EAGAIN || errno == EWOULDBLOCK)`
If you want a non-synthetic example, see https://github.com/BerkeleyLab/Bedrock in the badger/tests directory, where `make Vrtefi_blob` involves compiling ethernet_model.c in the Verilator context.

3. Build a modern Verilator from source, install, and use it for a build of the form described above.  No problem.

4. Rebuild and reinstall Verilator, this time with the --enable-ccwarn option provided to configure.  No warnings show up when compiling Verilator itself.  Good so far!  Try the above test case to exercise the resulting install.  Blam!
```
g++  -I. -Wall -Wextra -Wfloat-conversion -Wlogical-op -Werror -MMD -I/redacted/share/verilator/include -I/redacted/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=1 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-shadow      -std=gnu++14 -Os -c -o ethernet_model.o ../ethernet_model.c
../ethernet_model.c: In function ‘int ethernet_model(int, int, int*, int*, int)’:
../ethernet_model.c:99:26: error: logical ‘and’ of equal expressions [-Werror=logical-op]
    if ((errno != EAGAIN) && (errno != EWOULDBLOCK)) {
        ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
cc1plus: all warnings being treated as errors
make[1]: *** [Vrtefi_blob.mk:66: ethernet_model.o] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory '/redacted/bedrock/badger/tests/obj_dir'
make: *** [Makefile:285: Vrtefi_blob] Error 2
```

Fix (IMHO):  move -Wlogical-op from CFG_CXXFLAGS_WEXTRA to CFG_CXXFLAGS_SRC in configure.ac.  This doesn't change the compile options used for building verilator_bin itself: -Wlogical-op is still there if --enable-ccwarn is specified at configure-time.  But that gcc warning flag is no longer added to application build steps.  The resulting Verilator installation becomes actually useful for production!

Otherwise, --enable-ccwarn should be marked as test-only, not for installation and general use.  Personally, I like the test-like-you-fly and fly-like-you-test approach to building code.  And the more checks the compiler can provide, the better. Hence the previous suggestion.
